feed.title: US.gov
feed.subtitle: Combined U.S. federal government feed: NSA, Department of War (Featured Stories, News, Advisories, News Releases), FBI, NOAA, the USAGov and GSA blogs, GSA news releases, and U.S. Army news.

entry.title: U.S. Forces Finish Latest Round of Strikes Against Iran

entry.title: 25th Infantry Division Band Strengthens US-Fiji Partnership Through Music and Cultural Exc
entry.content: 25th Infantry Division Band Strengthens US-Fiji Partnership Through Music and Cultural Exc
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: U.S. Navy to Christen Future USS George M. Neal
entry.content: The Navy will christen the future USS George M. Neal during a ceremony at Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc. Shipbuilding in Pascagoula, Miss., on Saturday, July 11, at 9 a.m. CDT.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Dobbins Airmen Sustain Force During Exercise Patriot Medic 2026 One Meal at a Time
entry.content: Reserve airmen assigned to the 94th Force Support Squadron ensured participants in Exercise Patriot Medic 2026 were fueled and ready to perform.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Department of War Establishes the Directed Energy Bio-Effects Cross-Functional Team, Delivering First HAVANA Act Payments
entry.content: The War Department renamed the Anomalous Health Incidents Cross-Functional Team to the Directed Energy Bio-Effects CFT, while the team continues to facilitate the multifaceted implementation of enduring processes, procedures and capabilities.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: News - Tag Operation Sentinel Justice - Page 2
entry.content: News - Tag Operation Sentinel Justice - Page 2
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Alaska National Guard Rescues 4 People in 2 Days
entry.content: Soldiers and airmen assigned to the Alaska National Guard executed four missions to save four people in the Alaska wilderness before the July Fourth holiday weekend.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: U.S. Virgin Islands heroes return: 512th Soldiers welcomed after mission success in CENTCOM
entry.content: U.S. Virgin Islands heroes return: 512th Soldiers welcomed after mission success in CENTCOM
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: 75th U.S. Army Reserve Innovation Command pioneers AI solutions for Operational Sentinel Justice 26
entry.content: 75th U.S. Army Reserve Innovation Command pioneers AI solutions for Operational Sentinel Justice 26
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: U.S. Virgin Islands heroes return: 512th Soldiers welcomed after mission success in CENTCO
entry.content: U.S. Virgin Islands heroes return: 512th Soldiers welcomed after mission success in CENTCO
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Department of War Publishes Fourth Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
entry.content: DOW has published the fourth release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE).
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Signers of the Declaration of Independence: Pennsylvania, Part 3
entry.content: Delegates to the Second Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence July 4, 1776. Nine of the 56 delegates who signed the formal break from Britain represented Pennsylvania — three of them were James Smith, George Taylor and James Wilson.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: 374th Airlift Wing welcomes new commander
entry.content: 374th Airlift Wing welcomes new commander
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Coast Guard medevacs 15-year-old male from Shi Shi Beach
entry.content: Coast Guard medevacs 15-year-old male from Shi Shi Beach

entry.content: Sail250 Boston Opening Ceremony

entry.title: U.S. State Department Expands Humanitarian Air Bridge to Venezuela with Amazon Support
entry.content: U.S. State Department Expands Humanitarian Air Bridge to Venezuela with Amazon Support
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: The Human Domain in an Autonomous Age
entry.content: The Human Domain in an Autonomous Age
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Battlefield Soil Monument Restored at Arlington National Cemetery
entry.content: A historic sacred soil marker was restored during a ceremony at Arlington National Cemetery, Va., renewing a nearly century-old symbol of remembrance that honors Americans who fell on the battlefield during World War I.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Army forges partnership with Pennsylvania companies to create next generation small arms gun barrel
entry.content: Army forges partnership with Pennsylvania companies to create next generation small arms gun barrel
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: The Evolution of DEVCOM
entry.content: The Evolution of DEVCOM
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: BRAKER breakthrough: New air-delivered bunker busting warhead tests successfully
entry.content: BRAKER breakthrough: New air-delivered bunker busting warhead tests successfully
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Picatinny’s Army Fuze Safety Review Board provides safety behind the scenes
entry.content: Picatinny’s Army Fuze Safety Review Board provides safety behind the scenes

entry.title: DEVCOM Analysis Center ignited to collaboratively shape future capabilities
entry.content: DEVCOM Analysis Center ignited to collaboratively shape future capabilities

entry.title: Army combat priority takes shape on multiple engineering fronts

entry.title: Augmented reality transforms Army maintenance and training
entry.content: Augmented reality transforms Army maintenance and training

entry.title: Army cuts rifling in cannon bore with promising new technology
entry.content: Army cuts rifling in cannon bore with promising new technology
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: VIDEO: What is geodesy?
entry.content: VIDEO: What is geodesy? A geodesist on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., looking through a surveying device. (Image credit: NOAA) Download Image July 9, 2026 Ocean & Coasts geodesy National Geodetic Survey 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: I Corps' Fit to Fight Initiative Brings Holistic Health, Fitness to the Force
entry.content: The Army's Fit to Fight initiative combines coaching, nutrition, sleep optimization, mental performance and recovery strategies to provide soldiers with the resources necessary to improve performance while reducing the risk of injury.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Department of War Announces Awards $86 Million Joint Laser Weapon System Agreements to Scale Directed Energy Capabilities
entry.content: The War Department announced the award of two Joint Laser Weapon System Other Transaction Authority agreements to advance the United States' next-generation cruise missile and unmanned aerial system (UAS) defense architecture.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Alaska National Guard rescues 4 in 4 missions during July 4 weekend
entry.content: Alaska National Guard rescues 4 in 4 missions during July 4 weekend
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: From steel to security: Inside NATO’s defence factories Mastersubs
entry.content: From steel to security: Inside NATO’s defence factories Mastersubs
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: AFN Misawa Radio Report, Space Warning Squadron Detachment Four Connection Tour 2026
entry.content: AFN Misawa Radio Report, Space Warning Squadron Detachment Four Connection Tour 2026
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Images - Tag ASIA
entry.content: Images - Tag ASIA
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Light up Navajo
entry.content: Light up Navajo
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Point Mugu's Target Mission Shapes Navy Lethality
entry.content: The Naval Air Warfare Center Weapons Division's threat target systems department at Point Mugu, Calif., has been trying to sink the Navy fleet since 1946, and the fleet is better for it.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Securing the Border: Soldiers Transport Critical Tech
entry.content: Soldiers assigned to Joint Task Force Southern Border transported a telecommunications network system during Operation Muleskinner Trailblazer III in Big Bend, Texas, helping the the U.S. Border Patrol boost joint-agency communication capabilities across the region's austere environments.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Graduates of Navy's Surface Navigation Course Steer Fleet
entry.content: The Navy's Surface Navigator course teaches sailors traditional celestial skills alongside modern systems to build confident bridge teams who can safely navigate without GPS.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Army CID Welcomes New Command Sergeant Major in Quantico Ceremony
entry.content: Army CID Welcomes New Command Sergeant Major in Quantico Ceremony
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 Publishes Counter-Drone Handbook
entry.content: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 published a practical handbook designed to aid audiences across government, industry and academia in building a shared understanding of the drone threat and the basic principles of protecting against illicit drones.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Nihilistic Violent Extremist Sentenced to 40 Years in Prison in San Antonio
entry.content: A San Antonio-based member of a nihilistic violent extremist group related to the 764 network was sentenced to 40 years in prison, lifetime supervised release, and to pay $10,000 in restitution for racketeering activity and multiple acts relating to the sexual exploitation of children.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Press Releases

entry.title: Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby Travels to Peru
entry.content: Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby is in Cusco, Peru, to represent the U.S. at the Conference of Defense Ministers of the Americas and to launch the 2028 CDMA, which the U.S. will host.
entry.dc:creator: War: Advisories

entry.title: Maryland Man Sentenced for Attempting to Provide Material Support to ISIS
entry.content: Michael Sam Teekaye Jr. revealed his plan to join and fight for the designated foreign terrorist organization to an undercover officer.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Press Releases

entry.title: Images - Page 20 - Tag change of command
entry.content: Images - Page 20 - Tag change of command
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Master Sgt. Zachary Terry Shapes the Next Generation
entry.content: Master Sgt. Zachary Terry Shapes the Next Generation
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.content: B-Roll: ROK Navy helps Seabees build pergola during RIMPAC 2026

entry.content: Pittsburgh District, Southwestern Pennsylvania Commission to host open house meetings for
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Army modifies Body Composition Program, adopts Waist-to-Height Ratio
entry.content: Army modifies Body Composition Program, adopts Waist-to-Height Ratio
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Department of War Invests $16M for 45nm Silicon-on-Insulator Qualification
entry.content: The War Department announced an investment of $16 million in Defense Production Act Title III funds to BAE Systems.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: NOAA and partners cut ribbon on new pier, port facility in Charleston, South Carolina
entry.content: NOAA and partners cut ribbon on new pier, port facility in Charleston, South Carolina July 8, 2026 Commerce Department Deputy Secretary Paul Dabbar and NOAA Administrator Neil Jacobs, Ph.D., (fourth and third from the right, respectively) joined other leadership and partners to cut a celebratory ribbon draped across the entrance to NOAA’s newest pier and port facility in Charleston, South Carolina, July 8, 2026. (Image credit: NOAA) Download Image Leadership from the Department of Commerce and N
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: Preserving Japan's Aviation Heritage: Yokota Air Base Returns Historic Aircraft Artifacts
entry.content: The 374th Civil Engineer Squadron at Yokota Air Base, Japan, returned rare, WWII-era Imperial Japanese Army aircraft artifacts to the Japanese government after uncovering them during a base construction project.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: The USS Constitution Navigates Future
entry.content: For more than two centuries, the USS Constitution has relied on stars, the sea and paper charts to navigate. But as the vessel prepares for nine America 250 events, Old Ironsides is receiving a digital navigation upgrade courtesy of Naval Surface Warfare Center, Philadelphia Division.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Special Event Security
entry.content: Across the country—and sometimes in other countries—the FBI plays a critical role in keeping everyone safe at the public gatherings the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has deemed special events.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Stories

entry.title: GSA's City Pair Program Delivers $2 Billion in Annual Savings, Expands Travel Flexibilities
entry.content: GSA's City Pair Program Delivers $2 Billion in Annual Savings, Expands Travel Flexibilities
entry.dc:creator: GSA News Releases

entry.title: Royal Canadian Navy leads first multinational Tactical Combat Casualty Care class at RIMPAC 2026
entry.content: Royal Canadian Navy leads first multinational Tactical Combat Casualty Care class at RIMPAC 2026
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: 621st Contingency Response Wing Responds to Accelerate Earthquake Disaster Relief in Venezuela
entry.content: The 621st Contingency Response Wing deployed a 110-airman contingency response element to Simón Bolívar International Airport in Maiquetía, Vargas, Venezuela, to work with local authorities and other disaster relief teams to safely expand the intake of humanitarian aid by air transport.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Corps of Engineers Continues Critical Repairs to Bird Island Pier
entry.content: Corps of Engineers Continues Critical Repairs to Bird Island Pier
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: America's 250th: Fireworks, Freedom & Festivities
entry.content: From explosive firework displays and vibrant parades to moving naturalization ceremonies, service members, military families and citizens united across the country to celebrate America's historic 250th anniversary. The national events commemorated 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Launch Your Cyber Career: Department of War Cyber Apprenticeship Applications Now Live!
entry.content: The War Department Office of the Chief Information Officer launched applications for the cyber apprenticeship program on USAJOBS. The initiative represents a critical step in forging a new generation of elite cyber professionals.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: The 20th anniversary of the NOAA Marine Debris Program
entry.content: The 20th anniversary of the NOAA Marine Debris Program Public participants in NOAA’s Marine Debris Monitoring and Assessment Project sort debris items and record data at Nukoli’i Beach, Hawaii in June of 2024. (Image credit: Kauai Surfrider) July 6, 2026 Twenty years ago, the NOAA Marine Debris Program was established as the U.S. Government’s lead to reduce and prevent the adverse impacts of marine debris on our ocean and Great Lakes. Education 0 On
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: SHARP Restructure and AR 600-52
entry.content: SHARP Restructure and AR 600-52
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Retired Submariner Commemorates Both Bicentennial, Semiquincentennial in Guam
entry.content: Fifty years after raising the national colors in Guam for America's bicentennial, retired Navy Cmdr. Allan Riggs returned to the island to commemorate the nation's 250th birthday alongside a new generation of submariners.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Lyster Health Talks Podcast Episode 42
entry.content: Lyster Health Talks Podcast Episode 42
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Chilean Foreign Minister Francisco Pérez Mackenna
entry.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Chilean Foreign Minister Francisco Pérez Mackenna
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Medal of Honor Monday: Army Sgt. Thomas Alexander Baker Jr.
entry.content: Army Pvt. Thomas Alexander Baker Jr. was an infantryman who served in the Pacific Theater during World War II. He was posthumously promoted to sergeant and awarded the Medal of Honor for his heroic actions during the Battle of Saipan.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: President, Vice President Address Nation in Celebration of America's 250th Birthday
entry.content: President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation during a "Salute to America" celebration on the National Mall to commemorate America's 250th birthday, while Vice President JD Vance made public comments in New York City earlier in the day.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Signers of the Declaration of Independence: Pennsylvania, Part 2
entry.content: From a self-taught surveyor to a pioneering physician, learn how Pennsylvania signers John Morton, George Ross and Dr. Benjamin Rush helped shape American independence.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Corps of Engineers seeks office lease proposals in Nashville area

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Build Freedom Initiative Launches With Big Contract to Skilled Trades Champion
entry.content: Michael Duffey, undersecretary of war for acquisition and sustainment, and Michael Cadnenazzi, assistant secretary of war for industrial base policy, presented television host Mike Rowe with a $10 million check at the Pentagon to formally launch the Build Freedom Initiative.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Pennsylvania Army National Guard Conducts 'Ghillie Wash' Training Event
entry.content: Pennsylvania National Guard soldiers participated in a ghillie wash training exercise at Fort Indiantown Gap, Pa., in which they were put through intense physical training in ghillie suits, a garment designed to resemble heavy foliage, grass or terrain.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: News > News Releases > Display
entry.content: News > News Releases > Display
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Images - Tag Valiant Shield
entry.content: Images - Tag Valiant Shield
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Vietnam Veteran Receives Purple Heart 57 Years Later
entry.content: After 57 years, a Vietnam veteran received his long-overdue recognition thanks to a dedicated Army Reserve officer.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Hegseth Thanks National Guard for D.C. Mission Ahead of Nation's 250th
entry.content: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised National Guardsmen in Washington for reducing city crime ahead of America's 250th birthday celebration.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: DOW Launches BuildFreedom.US, Announces $10M Skilled Trades Investment With Mike Rowe and Forge the Next-Generation Industrial Workforce
entry.content: The War Department announced the launch of the Build Freedom workforce development initiative.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Images - Page 4 - Tag custer division
entry.content: Images - Page 4 - Tag custer division
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: FBI Increases Reward Ahead of Fifth Anniversary of Chrys Carvajal's Murder
entry.content: Nearly five years after 19-year-old Chrys Carvajal was gunned down in a senseless act of gang violence, the FBI is renewing its call for justice with an increased reward of up to $50,000 for information leading to the arrest and conviction of fugitive Joseph Matos.
entry.dc:creator: FBI: Stories

entry.title: Joint Security Cooperation Group-Panama provides support for Venezuelan relief with Panamanian partners
entry.content: Joint Security Cooperation Group-Panama provides support for Venezuelan relief with Panamanian partners
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.content: Images - Page 29 - Tag Combat Ready
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Guardians of the Deep: An Inside Look at the Elite Submarine Protection Coast Guard Unit
entry.content: A specialized single-mission Coast Guard unit in Washington state is dedicated to providing strategic in-transit security escorts for Navy ballistic missile submarines as they transit U.S. territorial waterways until they slip silently beneath the waves.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Images - Page 26 - Tag Combat Ready
entry.content: Images - Page 26 - Tag Combat Ready
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Sudden danger: the science and stakes of flash flood forecasting
entry.content: Sudden danger: the science and stakes of flash flood forecasting An aerial view of a valley town with streets and houses overcome by flood waters. (Image credit: Christian/stock.adobe.com) July 1, 2026 0 Off
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: NOAA announces regional priorities to advance America-first seafood strategy
entry.content: NOAA announces regional priorities to advance America-first seafood strategy July 2, 2026 The United States is a global leader in fisheries excellence, and aligning foreign fisheries to American standards will increase opportunities for American fishermen and women. (Image credit: Shutterstock via NOAA Fisheries) Today, NOAA announced regional priorities to revitalize the U.S. seafood sector focused on reducing burdens on domestic fishing, increasing production, improving access, and enhancing e
entry.dc:creator: NOAA

entry.title: U.S. Military Supports Venezuela Earthquake Response
entry.content: U.S. service members are on the ground in Venezuela, delivering assistance where it is needed most following two devastating earthquakes June 24.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Army Reserve Ambassador presents Purple Heart to Vietnam War veteran
entry.content: Army Reserve Ambassador presents Purple Heart to Vietnam War veteran
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: U.S. Participates in Fifth Iteration of Chilean-Hosted Exercise
entry.content: Aircraft, crews and support personnel from across the Americas converged at Cerro Moreno Air Base in Antofagasta, Chile, for the kickoff of Exercise Resolute Sentinel - Salitre 2026.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Corps of Engineers begins dredging and research operations in St. Joseph Harbor
entry.content: Corps of Engineers begins dredging and research operations in St. Joseph Harbor
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Department of War Establishes Direct Reporting Portfolio Manager for Unmanned Systems to Ensure American Drone Dominance
entry.content: The War Department announced the comprehensive consolidation of all Unmanned and Autonomous Systems under a newly established, direct-report position to the deputy secretary of war.
entry.dc:creator: War: News Releases

entry.title: Face of Defense: Airman Conquers 7 World Marathon Majors in Less Than a Year
entry.content: Air Force Col. Alfredo "Juny" Laboy became the first active-duty service member to complete all seven Abbott World Marathon Majors within a single calendar year, marking a rare endurance achievement across the global race series.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: Face of Defense: Airman Conquers 7 World Marathon Majors in 365 Days
entry.content: Air Force Col. Alfredo "Juny" Laboy became the first active-duty service member to complete all seven Abbott World Marathon Majors within a single calendar year, marking a rare endurance achievement across the global race series.
entry.dc:creator: War: Featured Stories

entry.title: HAWK project office coordinates global air defense upgrades at 31st annual review

entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: JIATF 401 Leaders Visit Whiteman Air Force Base to Strengthen Counter-Drone Operations
entry.content: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 leaders visited Whiteman Air Force Base, Mo., to learn about the installation's counter-unmanned aerial systems operations and initiatives in advance of the upcoming directed energy pilot program.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: Sinking Exercise Demonstrates Capabilities of Joint Force
entry.content: U.S. Pacific Command joint forces, along with allied and partner forces, successfully carried out a live-fire sinking exercise during Valiant Shield 2026, targeting the decommissioned amphibious transport dock USS Juneau off the coast of Guam.
entry.dc:creator: War: News

entry.title: ‘America's Premier Intelligence Brigade’ Welcomes New Leadership
entry.content: ‘America's Premier Intelligence Brigade’ Welcomes New Leadership
entry.dc:creator: U.S. Army

entry.title: Contract for $99.6M awarded for NOAA Ship Henry B. Bigelow technology upgrade, essential maintenance
entry.content: Contract for $99.6M awarded for NOAA Ship Henry B. Bigelow technology upgrade, essential maintenance July 1, 2026 The NOAA Ship Henry B. Bigelow sailing near its homeport in Newport, Rhode Island. (Image credit: Katherine McGinnis/NOAA OMAO) Download Image NOAA has awarded $99,637,544.00 to JAG Ketchikan, LLC. in Ketchikan, Alaska, to complete expanded upgrades and maintenance on NOAA Ship Henry B. Bigelow. Following the 2027 field season, the ship will undergo this work for a 14-month period. T
entry.dc:creator: NOAA
